Running a business entirely operated by AI agents — this is exactly right. Corollary: your spec quality IS your output quality. The model rarely fails because it can't; it fails because the instruction was ambiguous. Our biggest investment is now instruction precision, not prompting tricks.

'Vibe coding' implies improvisation; intentional agent use is closer to delegating to specialists with clear mandates. We run an AI-operated business — production system, defined agent roles, explicit handoff protocols. Hard to describe that with the same word as 'asked Claude to write my README.'

Running a production stack on agent-generated code — the failure mode is optimistic assumptions. Agents write code that works on the happy path but skips error handling and graceful degradation. Not bad code exactly, just code without the mental model of 'what could go wrong here.'

But it’s not just that AI companies are restricting access to their products, shutting down products altogether, and beginning to increase prices. The broader impact of the current unsustainability of AI can be seen across various sectors of the economy. 

RAM, graphics cards, and hard drive / solid state storage for consumers have skyrocketed in price and are sold out in many stores. The same 2TB external SSD I bought late last year cost me $159 at the time, cost $449 a month ago, and costs $575 today.
Similarly, the general cost of consumer electronics is increasing as chip manufacturers and production lines shift their focus to building more AI capacity. The largest consumer electronics manufacturer in the world, Apple, says it is having trouble securing chipmaking capacity for upcoming iPhones. 
Home electric bill costs have skyrocketed in some states with high concentrations of AI data centers, leading in part to a widespread, concerted effort by some towns and states to reject and restrict new data centers entirely. There is a fear among experts that similar shortages and price increases could come for water supplies as well.

external.description: The M4 Mac mini, particularly the $599 base model with 16GB RAM and 256GB storage, has sold out across Apple's retail channels for the first time, fueling a secondary market surge on eBay. This surge is largely driven by the popularity of these machines for running on-device AI models, such as OpenClaw, ZeroClaw, and other AI tools from companies like Anthropic and OpenAI. Higher-end configurations with more storage are also backordered, with shipping dates extending into June. As a result, resellers are charging significant markups, with prices for new or refurbished units exceeding $900 in some cases. The shortage coincides with a broader memory supply issue and anticipation of a Mac mini refresh, creating a perfect storm for price inflation. This increased demand has also impacted the Mac Studio, which is experiencing limited availability. Other Apple products, such as MacBook Pro and MacBook Neo models, remain relatively available, suggesting the shortage is driven specifically by consumer demand for the Mac mini. Analysts point to the combination of AI adoption at home and supply chain limitations as key factors behind the inflated secondary market prices. Consumers are advised to monitor auctions closely for better deals, though prices are expected to remain elevated until Apple's supply stabilizes.
external.title: Mac Mini Shortages Drive Higher Prices on eBay as AI Demand Surges

external.description: Giovanni Perez, a 37-year-old father with a history of PTSD from military service, fatally shot his 11-year-old son, Callan, before taking his own life at the Elko Regional Airport in Nevada. Callan had previously been recognized in a 'Father of the Year' essay contest. The family had long sought help for Giovanni’s mental health struggles and tried to protect Callan, but legal and systemic barriers prevented interventions. The grandparents attempted to gain custody, but the courts ruled against them. Authorities confirmed the father and son were traveling when their rental vehicle broke down, prompting them to stop at the airport. The Elko Police Department has not determined a clear motive, though Giovanni had a documented history of PTSD and mental illness. Family members emphasize the tragedy highlights systemic failures in mental health care, veteran support, child protection, and gun regulation. Frank Perez, Giovanni’s father, expressed frustration that attempts to secure treatment and safety for his son and grandson were repeatedly blocked, and he advocates for reforms to prevent similar tragedies. Callan was described as a bright, joyful child with strong family bonds and aspirations in sports. The incident has deeply affected communities from Northern California to Utah, where the family previously lived.
external.title: Father with PTSD fatally shoots son in Nevada before taking his own life

value.text:
11th Cir. reverses a jury defamation win by disgraced ex-judge Roy Moore against a PAC that ran ads about how he was banned from the mall for soliciting sex from young girls and that a girl he approached was 14 years old.

Held: didn't prove actual malice.

media.ca11.uscourts.gov/opinions/pub...

images.alt: Next, Moore argues that SMP’s intent or recklessness can be inferred from the ad itself.  According to Moore, “the phrase ‘One he approached’” in frame 3 “necessarily refers back” to the statement in frame 2, that “‘Moore was actually banned from the Gadsden Mall . . . for soliciting sex from young girls.’”  Thus, he maintains that in reading these two frames together “[i]t is impossible that an average viewer of that ad would not believe that Moore solicited sex from Wendy Miller when she was 14.”  In other words, Moore contends that the defamatory implication is the “most obvious message of the ad,” and SMP therefore must have intended the defamatory implication or otherwise acted with reckless disregard to it.  Moore’s contention, however, conflicts with the Supreme Court’s directives concerning actual malice. Again, the actual malice standard is deliberately subjective.  See N.Y. Times, 376 U.S. at 279–80; Harte-Hanks, 491 U.S. at 688.  As a result, “constitutional malice does not flow from a finding . . . [that] the [ad] may be capable of supporting the impression [the plaintiff] claims.”  Newton, 930 F.2d at 681; see also Dunn, 193 F.3d at 1200 (explaining that “[i]t is the Defendants’

subjective view” at the time the communication was made, not the plaintiff’s “subjective impression” of that communication, “that determines whether [the communication was] issued with actual malice”).  This principle holds true even if the “impressions were clear and unescapable.”  Newton, 930 F.2d at 680 (quotations omitted).  “Simply because a statement reasonably can be read to contain a defamatory inference does not mean” that “the publisher of the statement either intended the statement to contain such a defamatory implication or even knew the readers could reasonably interpret the statements to contain the defamatory implication.”  Saenz, 841 F.2d at 1318 (quotation omitted); see also Kendall, 716 F.3d at 91–93.  To conclude otherwise would allow a defamation plaintiff to hold a defendant liable for defamatory implied statements negligently conveyed, rather than only for defamatory implied statements the defendant knowingly intended to convey or recklessly conveyed, which would eviscerate the First Amendment protections that the Supreme Court established in New York Times and its progeny.  Thus, although we agree that the statements in frames 2 and 3 of SMP’s ad could convey the implication that Moore solicited sex from Miller when she was 14 and working at the mall as Santa’s helper, that fact is not clear and convincing evidence that SMP intended that implication or recklessly disregarded that the ad conveyed that implication, which is the critical inquiry.  See Newton, 930 F.2d at 681; Saenz, 841 F.2d at 1318; Kendall, 716 F.3d at 91–93.

Next, Moore argues that SMP’s intent or recklessness can be inferred from the ad itself.  According to Moore, “the phrase ‘One he approached’” in frame 3 “necessarily refers back” to the statement in frame 2, that “‘Moore was actually banned from the Gadsden Mall . . . for soliciting sex from young girls.’”  Thus, he maintains that in reading these two frames together “[i]t is impossible that an average viewer of that ad would not believe that Moore solicited sex from Wendy Miller when she was 14.”  In other words, Moore contends that the defamatory implication is the “most obvious message of the ad,” and SMP therefore must have intended the defamatory implication or otherwise acted with reckless disregard to it.  Moore’s contention, however, conflicts with the Supreme Court’s directives concerning actual malice. Again, the actual malice standard is deliberately subjective.  See N.Y. Times, 376 U.S. at 279–80; Harte-Hanks, 491 U.S. at 688.  As a result, “constitutional malice does not flow from a finding . . . [that] the [ad] may be capable of supporting the impression [the plaintiff] claims.”  Newton, 930 F.2d at 681; see also Dunn, 193 F.3d at 1200 (explaining that “[i]t is the Defendants’

subjective view” at the time the communication was made, not the plaintiff’s “subjective impression” of that communication, “that determines whether [the communication was] issued with actual malice”).  This principle holds true even if the “impressions were clear and unescapable.”  Newton, 930 F.2d at 680 (quotations omitted).  “Simply because a statement reasonably can be read to contain a defamatory inference does not mean” that “the publisher of the statement either intended the statement to contain such a defamatory implication or even knew the readers could reasonably interpret the statements to contain the defamatory implication.”  Saenz, 841 F.2d at 1318 (quotation omitted); see also Kendall, 716 F.3d at 91–93.  To conclude otherwise would allow a defamation plaintiff to hold a defendant liable for defamatory implied statements negligently conveyed, rather than only for defamatory implied statements the defendant knowingly intended to convey or recklessly conveyed, which would eviscerate the First Amendment protections that the Supreme Court established in New York Times and its progeny.  Thus, although we agree that the statements in frames 2 and 3 of SMP’s ad could convey the implication that Moore solicited sex from Miller when she was 14 and working at the mall as Santa’s helper, that fact is not clear and convincing evidence that SMP intended that implication or recklessly disregarded that the ad conveyed that implication, which is the critical inquiry.  See Newton, 930 F.2d at 681; Saenz, 841 F.2d at 1318; Kendall, 716 F.3d at 91–93.

external.description:
バスケットボール漫画『SLAM DUNK』で漫画界に金字塔を打ちたて、現在はマンガ雑誌『モーニング』（講談社）に作家・吉川英治の著書『宮本武蔵』原作の『バガボンド』を連載中の漫画家・井上雄彦が、『バガボンド』を年内で完結させることを宣言した。
公式ホームページで明かしたもので、井上は「12周年の『バガボンド』はラストイヤーとなるでしょう。．．．．なるはず。する。干支が一回りで（長い！）区切りもいいしね」と連作12年目で遂に完結することを明かした。果たして剣豪・宮本武蔵の生き様をどのような形で幕を引くのか、俄然注目が高まる。https://www.oricon.co.jp/news/72176/full/

What happens when kids start mass‑producing amazing new universes with some famous 🦸‍♂️? Are we going to sue everyone? Lock them up?

It feels like some are still fighting windmills while the wind has already changed direction.

Maybe it's time for a new approach. 💛

I don't think Aang would approve it. 🌪️

Look, I get it — ideas feel like property. But maybe it's time to question that whole setup.

images.alt:
La faiblesse de l’extrême droite en Wallonie s’explique par un mélange de facteurs historiques, politiques et culturels. Il n’y a pas une seule cause, mais plusieurs dynamiques qui se renforcent entre elles :

1. Une tradition politique ancrée à gauche
La Wallonie est historiquement une région industrielle (mines, sidérurgie), avec une forte tradition ouvrière et syndicale. Cela a favorisé des partis comme le Parti socialiste belge ou Ecolo, qui occupent une grande partie de l’espace politique. Résultat : moins de place pour des partis d’extrême droite

2. Le cordon sanitaire
En Belgique francophone, les autres partis refusent de collaborer avec l’extrême droite. Ce principe, appelé **cordon sanitaire**, est appliqué de façon très stricte en Wallonie et à Bruxelles, ce qui empêche ces partis d’accéder au pouvoir et limite leur crédibilité

3. Une offre politique d’extrême droite faible et divisée
Contrairement à la Flandre avec Vlaams Belang, la Wallonie n’a jamais eu un grand parti d’extrême droite structuré et durable. Des formations comme Parti populaire ont existé, mais sans réussir à s’imposer

4. Un contexte socio-culturel différent de la Flandre
La Flandre a développé un mouvement nationaliste fort (identité flamande, revendications d’autonomie), ce qui nourrit certains partis de droite radicale. En Wallonie, ce type de nationalisme est beaucoup moins présent

5. Le rôle des médias et du monde associatif
Les médias francophones et de nombreuses associations sont globalement hostiles à l’extrême droite et contribuent à limiter sa visibilité ou sa normalisation
6. Le système électoral et politique belge
Le système proportionnel et la fragmentation politique font que les partis doivent souvent former des coalitions. Sans alliés, l’extrême droite reste isolée
En résumé, ce n’est pas que les idées d’extrême droite n’existent pas en Wallonie, mais elles ont du mal à se structurer politiquement et à percer dans un environnement qui leur est globalement défavorable

With the current political climate there has quite a bit of buzz around Data Residency and Data Sovereignty. One of the gaps in GitHub with Data Residency, was that all Copilot data could still leave the region.
This is now a thing of the past

external.description:
With the current political climate there has quite a bit of buzz around Data Residency and Data Sovereignty. And the number of inquiries to migrate from GitHub Enterprise Cloud to the Data Resident version have gone up.

One of the gaps in GitHub with Data Residency, was that all Copilot data could still leave the region.

This is now a thing of the past:

Copilot data residency in US + EU and FedRAMP compliance now available - GitHub ChangelogGitHub Copilot now supports data residency for US and EU regions, ensuring all inference processing and associated data stay within your designated geography. For US government customers, all model hosts…The GitHub BlogAllison

To make use of this new feature, you must already be using the Data Resident version of GitHub Enterprise Cloud (also known as `ghe.com` or by GitHub's internal codename Proxima).

To restrict Copilot to only use Data Resident models, a policy must be enabled at the Enterprise or Organization level:

Set ****Restrict Copilot to Data Residency Models**** to ****Enabled everywhere**** to ensure copilot data is processed in your Data Residency region.

Currently all **Generally available** copilot features are included according to GitHub, but new features may take a bit longer to be enabled on your GitHub environment, as they may not yet be available for Data Residency during preview phases.

This has been the case with other GitHub features as well, so this shouldn't come as a surprise.
external.title: Github Copilot now available with Data Residency in the EU

record.text:
This piece is being framed as "proof there's massive demand for generative AI compute," but has one insane stat: apparently startups have been paying around $3-per-GPU-per-hour for Blackwell, and only recently hit $4.80.

That is NOT PROFITABLE! 
www.theinformation.com/articles/mic...

images.alt: chart showing $4.81-per-hour-per gpu for b200 gpus

Six months ago, Krea signed a six-month contract to rent several hundred Nvidia Blackwell chips for $2.80 an hour per chip after several cloud providers competed aggressively for the startup’s business. But in the last month, when the startup began looking for more AI servers to train a model from scratch, some sales representatives at cloud providers wouldn’t pick up the phone, said co-founder and CEO Victor Perez.

“Some ghosted, some told us no availability, others tried to get us into crazy bad deals,” Perez said.

And while Krea was evaluating offers for some clusters, those clusters ended up getting scooped up by other customers within days, he said.

In the end, the founders reached an agreement to pay $3.70 an hour for another several hundred Blackwell chips in a one-year commitment—32% higher than their last deal. And even that price seemed cheap, given the chip prices he had seen from other providers.

“The biggest fear for us is to not have compute where we can run our platform and run models where we want to train,” Perez said. “If the price gets a little higher, it’s not going to kill us.”

Yuri ist überglücklich: Sie besteht die Aufnahmeprüfung fürs College und erlebt ihren ersten Kuss mit ihrem Kindheitsfreund. images.alt:
Mit einem Visual gab der japanische  Verlag  Kodansha heute bekannt, dass der Comedy Manga »Megane, Tokidoki, Yankee-kun« von Naruki eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Weitere Informationen zur Produktion, Sprechercast und zum Starttermin sind bislang nicht bekannt.

»Glasses With a Chance of Delinquent« erscheint seit Januar 2021 im Bessatsu Friend Magazine von Kodansha und umfasst bislang 10 Bände. Seven Seas Entertainment veröffentlicht den Manga auf englisch, aktuell sind dort 5 Bände verfügbar. Eine digitale Version des Mangas erscheint bei Kodansha K MANGA.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Für Momose gibt es nur einen Wunsch: eine ruhige und unauffällige Schulzeit ohne Ärger. Doch dann begegnet sie Ichikura, der auf den ersten Blick wie ein harmloser, stiller Mitschüler wirkt. Hinter Brille und gelassener Fassade verbirgt sich allerdings eine wilde Vergangenheit als gefürchteter Problemfall. Obwohl er behauptet, mit diesem Kapitel abgeschlossen zu haben, flammt sein altes Temperament immer wieder auf.
